threat_model: SCCML trace evidence can accidentally become live kernel execution, subprocess execution, external repository reads, replay, state mutation, proof commitment, governance proof acceptance, unsupported-operation silence, raw trace or state retention, or terminal closure if evidence and authority boundaries are not explicit. The mitigation is a witness-only schema and validator that reject raw retention and deny all runtime and proof-admission authority in Foundation Mode.

required_checks.check: SCCML trace witness live kernel execution, subprocess, external repository read, replay, mutation, proof commitment, proof acceptance, connector, write, terminal, and success authority remain false
redaction check confirms trace evidence remains digest refs and raw trace, raw state, and raw secret retention remain denied
IDOR check confirms tenant scope stays foundation-local-only and adapter mode remains witness-only operator-supplied refs
receipt integrity check confirms KernelProof, TraceEntry, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms unsupported-operation gaps cannot be silently ignored
audit visibility check confirms the proof coverage matrix includes the SCCML trace adapter witness surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode witness blocks live kernel execution, subprocess execution, external repository reads, instruction replay, state mutation, proof commitment, governance proof acceptance, connector calls, writes, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode witness requires digest refs, state hash refs, raw trace and raw state retention denial, raw secret denial, private payload redaction, and operator review.

mitigations: keep SccmlTraceAdapterWitness validation non-live and deterministic
require digest refs instead of raw instruction traces, state payloads, proof payloads, or adapter manifests
require KernelProof, TraceEntry, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs
deny live SCCML execution, subprocess execution, replay, state mutation, and proof acceptance
deny unsupported-operation silence, connector calls, external writes, terminal closure, and success claims in Foundation Mode
